1: use anyhow::{Result, anyhow, bail};
2: use chrono::Utc;
3: use iban::IbanLike;
4: use num_traits::ToPrimitive;
5: use quick_xml::Writer;
6: use quick_xml::events::Event as XmlEvent;
7: use quick_xml::events::{BytesDecl, BytesEnd, BytesStart, BytesText};
8: use tracing::warn;
9: use uuid::Uuid;
10: 
11: use crate::error::ValidationError;
12: use crate::models::{Event, EventSubscription};
13: 
14: pub(crate) fn validate_iban(raw: &str) -> Result<iban::Iban, ValidationError> {
15:     let normalized = raw.to_uppercase();
16:     normalized.parse().map_err(|_| {
17:         warn!("IBAN validation failed for input: {}", normalized);
18:         ValidationError::new("Bitte gib eine gültige IBAN ein.")
19:     })
20: }
21: 
22: pub(crate) fn validate_iban_str(raw: &str) -> Result<String, ValidationError> {
23:     Ok(validate_iban(raw)?.electronic_str().to_string())
24: }
25: 
26: pub(crate) async fn lookup_bic(iban: &str) -> Result<String> {
27:     let parsed = iban
28:         .parse::<iban::Iban>()
29:         .map_err(|_| anyhow!("Invalid IBAN: {}", iban))?;
30: 
31:     if let Some(bank_code) = parsed.bank_identifier()
32:         && let Some(bank) = fints_institute_db::get_bank_by_bank_code(bank_code)
33:     {
34:         return Ok(bank.bic.to_string());
35:     }
36: 
37:     let url = format!("https://bankcheck.dev/api/v1/validate?q={}", iban);
38:     let client = reqwest::Client::new();
39:     let response = client
40:         .get(&url)
41:         .send()
42:         .await
43:         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("BIC lookup failed: {}", e))?;
44: 
45:     let json: serde_json::Value = response
46:         .json()
47:         .await
48:         .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to parse BIC response: {}", e))?;
49: 
50:     if let Some(bic) = json
51:         .get("result")
52:         .and_then(|r| r.get("bankInfo"))
53:         .and_then(|bi| bi.get("bic"))
54:         .and_then(|b| b.as_str())
55:         && !bic.is_empty()
56:     {
57:         return Ok(bic.to_string());
58:     }
59: 
60:     bail!("No BIC found for IBAN: {}", iban)
61: }
62: 
63: fn write_element(writer: &mut Writer<Vec<u8>>, name: &str, value: &str) -> Result<()> {
64:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new(name)))?;
65:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Text(BytesText::new(value)))?;
66:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new(name)))?;
67:     Ok(())
68: }
69: 
70: fn write_element_with_attr(
71:     writer: &mut Writer<Vec<u8>>,
72:     name: &str,
73:     attr: (&str, &str),
74:     text: &str,
75: ) -> Result<()> {
76:     let mut elem = BytesStart::new(name);
77:     elem.push_attribute(attr);
78:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(elem))?;
79:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Text(BytesText::new(text)))?;
80:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new(name)))?;
81:     Ok(())
82: }
83: 
84: pub(crate) fn generate_sepa_xml(
85:     event: &Event,
86:     bookings: &[(EventSubscription, String)],
87:     creditor_name: &str,
88:     creditor_iban: &str,
89:     creditor_bic: &str,
90: ) -> Result<String> {
91:     let mut writer = Writer::new_with_indent(Vec::new(), b' ', 2);
92: 
93:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Decl(BytesDecl::new("1.0", Some("UTF-8"), None)))?;
94: 
95:     let doc_attrs = vec![
96:         ("xmlns", "urn:iso:std:iso:20022:tech:xsd:pain.008.001.02"),
97:         ("xmlns:xsi", "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"),
98:     ];
99:     let mut doc = BytesStart::new("Document");
100:     for (k, v) in &doc_attrs {
101:         doc.push_attribute((*k, *v));
102:     }
103:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(doc.clone()))?;
104: 
105:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("CstmrDrctDbtInitn")))?;
106: 
107:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("GrpHdr")))?;
108:     let msg_id = Uuid::new_v4().simple().to_string();
109:     write_element(&mut writer, "MsgId", &msg_id)?;
110:     write_element(
111:         &mut writer,
112:         "CreDtTm",
113:         &Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S").to_string(),
114:     )?;
115:     write_element(&mut writer, "NbOfTxs", &bookings.len().to_string())?;
116: 
117:     let ctrl_sum: f64 = bookings
118:         .iter()
119:         .map(|(sub, _)| sub.total_price(event).to_f64().unwrap_or(0.0))
120:         .sum();
121:     write_element(&mut writer, "CtrlSum", &format!("{:.2}", ctrl_sum))?;
122: 
123:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("InitgPty")))?;
124:     write_element(&mut writer, "Nm", creditor_name)?;
125:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("InitgPty")))?;
126:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("GrpHdr")))?;
127: 
128:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("PmtInf")))?;
129:     let pmt_inf_id = Uuid::new_v4().simple().to_string();
130:     write_element(&mut writer, "PmtInfId", &pmt_inf_id)?;
131:     write_element(&mut writer, "PmtMtd", "DD")?;
132:     write_element(&mut writer, "NbOfTxs", &bookings.len().to_string())?;
133:     write_element(&mut writer, "CtrlSum", &format!("{:.2}", ctrl_sum))?;
134: 
135:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("PmtTpInf")))?;
136:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("SvcLvl")))?;
137:     write_element(&mut writer, "Cd", "SEPA")?;
138:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("SvcLvl")))?;
139:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("LclInstrm")))?;
140:     write_element(&mut writer, "Cd", "CORE")?;
141:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("LclInstrm")))?;
142:     write_element(&mut writer, "SeqTp", "FRST")?;
143:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("PmtTpInf")))?;
144: 
145:     write_element(
146:         &mut writer,
147:         "ReqdColltnDt",
148:         &Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string(),
149:     )?;
150: 
151:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("Cdtr")))?;
152:     write_element(&mut writer, "Nm", creditor_name)?;
153:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("Cdtr")))?;
154: 
155:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("CdtrAcct")))?;
156:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("Id")))?;
157:     write_element(&mut writer, "IBAN", creditor_iban)?;
158:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("Id")))?;
159:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("CdtrAcct")))?;
160: 
161:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("CdtrAgt")))?;
162:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("FinInstnId")))?;
163:     write_element(&mut writer, "BIC", creditor_bic)?;
164:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("FinInstnId")))?;
165:     writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("CdtrAgt")))?;
166: 
167:     write_element(&mut writer, "ChrgBr", "SLEV")?;
168: 
169:     for (sub, bic) in bookings {
170:         let price = sub.total_price(event);
171:         let mandate_ref = format!("SEPA-{}", sub.payment_id);
172:         let sign_date = sub.created.format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string();
173: 
174:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("DrctDbtTxInf")))?;
175: 
176:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("PmtId")))?;
177:         write_element(&mut writer, "EndToEndId", &mandate_ref)?;
178:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::End(BytesEnd::new("PmtId")))?;
179: 
180:         write_element_with_attr(
181:             &mut writer,
182:             "InstdAmt",
183:             ("Ccy", "EUR"),
184:             &format!("{:.2}", price),
185:         )?;
186: 
187:         writer.write_event(XmlEvent::Start(BytesStart::new("DrctDbtTx")))?;

# SEPA XML Format Analysis

## Comparison: working file vs. our generated file

The working file (`2026-07-17_CDC_...xml`) is from a bank/tooling that conforms to the SEPA CORE rulebook. Our generated file (`sepa-sepa-test-kurs-...xml`) has technical XSD-valid XML but is missing several elements that bank software commonly enforces.

### Critical differences (most likely cause of rejection)

| # | Element | Working file | Our file | XSD | SEPA rulebook |
|---|---------|--------------|----------|-----|----------------|
| 1 | `<CdtrSchmeId>` | **Present** (DE54ZZZ00000299406) | **Missing** | optional | **required** for CORE |
| 2 | `<CdtrAcct>/<Ccy>` | **`<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>`** | **Missing** | optional | recommended |
| 3 | `<SeqTp>` | `RCUR` (recurring) | hardcoded `FRST` (first) | optional | must match mandate type |
| 4 | `<CtgyPurp>` | `<Cd>OTHR</Cd>` | **Missing** | optional | recommended |
| 5 | `<BtchBookg>` | `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>` | **Missing** | optional | recommended |

### Minor / less likely to be the cause

| # | Element | Working file | Our file |
|---|---------|--------------|----------|
| 6 | `<CreDtTm>` | `2026-04-28T18:35:13Z` (with Z) | `2026-07-11T11:37:56` (no tz) |
| 7 | `<Document xsi:schemaLocation>` | present | missing |
| 8 | `<MndtRltdInf>/<AmdmntInd>` | `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>` | missing |
| 9 | `<MsgId>` / `<PmtInfId>` | `1120325-0` / `20260428-48346-339-1` (formatted) | UUIDs |

### Source locations

- XML generation: `src/logic/banking.rs:84-231` (`generate_sepa_xml`)
- Hardcoded FRST: `src/logic/banking.rs:142`
- Orchestration: `src/logic/events.rs:251-324` (`export_sepa_xml`)
- Secrets currently: only name + IBAN (`src/logic/secrets.rs:80-82`) — no creditor scheme ID stored
- Existing test: `src/logic/banking.rs:343-354` validates against XSD only (passes — but doesn't catch rulebook gaps)

## Recommended plan

1. **Add creditor scheme ID configuration**
   - Add a third secret `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` (e.g. `DE54ZZZ00000299406`) to `src/logic/secrets.rs` (`SepaCreditorId` variant, env key, getter)
   - Add it to `ConfigIncomplete` check in `export_sepa_xml`

2. **Write `<CdtrSchmeId>` block** in `generate_sepa_xml` after `<ChrgBr>` (and before the transaction loop). Use `Othr` + `SchmeNm/Prtry=SEPA` to mirror the working file.

3. **Write `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>`** inside `<CdtrAcct>` after the `<Id>` close.

4. **Make `<SeqTp>` configurable**
   - Add a `sepa_sequence_type` (or `sepa_type`) field to `Event` model + migration (e.g. `FRST` for first, `RCUR` for recurring, `FNAL` for last, `OOFF` for one-off) — or simpler: accept it as a parameter on the export endpoint
   - Pass it through `export_sepa_xml` → `generate_sepa_xml` and use it instead of hardcoded `FRST`

5. **Add `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>`** after `<SeqTp>` in `PmtTpInf`. Could be a constant or a per-event config like the sequence type.

6. **Add `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`** right after `<PmtMtd>` in `PmtInf`.

7. **Fix `<CreDtTm>` formatting** — use `%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` (UTC) in `src/logic/banking.rs:113`.

8. **Add `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>`** inside `<MndtRltdInf>` (after `<DtOfSgntr>`) at `src/logic/banking.rs:190-191`.

9. **Add `xsi:schemaLocation`** to `<Document>` at `src/logic/banking.rs:95-98`.

10. **Extend tests** in `src/logic/banking.rs`:
    - Assert the new elements are present
    - Add a second test that verifies `<CdtrSchmeId>` content with the expected CI format
    - Keep the XSD validation test (it should still pass since all new fields are rulebook-required, not XSD-required)

content.text:
# Final Plan: Fix SEPA XML Format

## Decisions locked in
- Creditor ID: new env secret `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` (alongside existing `SEPA_CREDITOR_NAME`, `SEPA_CREDITOR_IBAN`)
- `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>` and `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>` hardcoded
- Include all polish: `xsi:schemaLocation`, `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`, `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>`, Z-suffix on `<CreDtTm>`

## Files to change

### 1. `src/logic/secrets.rs`
- Add `SepaCreditorId` to `SecretKey` enum
- Add `#[serde(default, rename = "SEPA_CREDITOR_ID")] sepa_creditor_id: String` to the secrets struct
- Add match arm `SecretKey::SepaCreditorId => Ok(secrets.sepa_creditor_id)`

### 2. `src/logic/banking.rs:84` — extend `generate_sepa_xml` signature
- Add `creditor_id: &str` parameter (the `DE..ZZZ...` value)
- Inside the function, in order:
  - Add `xsi:schemaLocation` attribute to `<Document>` (line 95-98)
  - Change `CreDtTm` format to `%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` (line 113)
  - Add `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>` after `<PmtMtd>` (after line 131)
  - Change `<SeqTp>FRST</SeqTp>` → `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>` (line 142)
  - Add `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>` after `<SeqTp>` (after line 142)
  - Add `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>` inside `<CdtrAcct>` after the closing `</Id>` (after line 158)
  - Add `<CdtrSchmeId>...</CdtrSchmeId>` block after `<ChrgBr>` (after line 167) with `PrvtId` → `Othr` → `Id=creditor_id` + `SchmeNm/Prtry=SEPA`
  - Add `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>` inside `<MndtRltdInf>` after `<DtOfSgntr>` (after line 190)

### 3. `src/logic/banking.rs:264` — update existing test
- Pass `creditor_id` to `generate_sepa_xml` in both test calls
- Add assertions for the new elements (`<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`, `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>`, `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>`, `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>`, `<CdtrSchmeId>...DE..ZZZ...</CdtrSchmeId>`, `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>`, Z-suffix on `CreDtTm`, `xsi:schemaLocation` on `Document`)
- Keep the XSD validation block (still expected to pass)

### 4. `src/logic/events.rs:266-271` — plumb creditor_id and update incomplete-config check
- Read `secrets.get(SecretKey::SepaCreditorId)`
- Treat empty creditor_id the same as empty name/iban (extend `ConfigIncomplete` error)
- Pass `creditor_id` to `banking::generate_sepa_xml` at line 304

### 5. `.env` (local dev) and AWS parameter store (prod) — out of repo
- Add `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID=DE54ZZZ00000299406` (or whatever the actual assigned CI is) locally
- Same value to the secrets store consumed by the Lambda

## Open follow-up (out of scope for this fix)

- Membership-style events currently share the same export path as test/event-style bookings. The working file in the repo (`2026-07-17_CDC_...xml`) is clearly a membership run, while the test export is a one-off payment. Once this fix lands, decide whether `generate_sepa_xml` should branch by event type (FRST/RCUR, OTHR/OTHR, etc.) or move the SeqTp/CtgyPurp to a per-event column.
- The membership file uses `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>` on the creditor account and has every transaction with an explicit BIC. The current code looks up the BIC per booking via `banking::lookup_bic` — if any booking BIC is missing, the whole export fails. Consider whether to fall back to `NOTPROVIDED` per the SEPA rulebook for known BICs.
- `<MsgId>` and `<PmtInfId>` are still UUIDs. The working file uses formatted IDs. SEPA accepts both; not changing unless you want it.

## Verification
- `cargo test` — both new banking tests pass, XSD validation still passes
- `cargo clippy --all-features`
- `cargo fmt`
- Re-export a SEPA file via the API, diff the structure against `~/Downloads/2026-07-17_CDC_*.xml`

Ready to switch out of plan mode and implement on your go.

87: /// Reads the consolidated AWS secret, falling back to environment variables.
88: /// Owns its own cache and lazily-initialized AWS client.
89: pub(crate) struct ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider {
90:     env: EnvSecretProvider,
91:     cache: Cache<String, Secrets>,
92:     client: tokio::sync::OnceCell<Client>,
93: }
94: 
95: impl ConsolidatedAwsSecretProvider {
96:     pub(crate) fn new() -> Self {
97:         Self {
98:             env: EnvSecretProvider,
99:             cache: Cache::builder()
100:                 .time_to_live(Duration::from_secs(900)) // 15 minutes
101:                 .build(),
102:             client: tokio::sync::OnceCell::new(),
103:         }
104:     }
105: 
106:     async fn client(&self) -> Result<&Client> {
107:         self.client
108:             .get_or_try_init(|| async {
109:                 let config = aws_config::defaults(BehaviorVersion::latest())
110:                     .region(Region::new(
111:                         env::var("AWS_REGION").unwrap_or_else(|_| "eu-central-1".to_string()),
112:                     ))
113:                     .load()
114:                     .await;
115:                 Ok::<Client, anyhow::Error>(Client::new(&config))
116:             })
117:             .await
118:     }
119: 
120:     async fn load_secrets(&self) -> Result<Secrets> {
121:         let secret_name = CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME.to_string();
122:         if let Some(secrets) = self.cache.get(&secret_name) {
123:             return Ok(secrets);
124:         }
125: 
126:         let client = self.client().await?;
127:         let resp = client
128:             .get_secret_value()
129:             .secret_id(CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME)
130:             .send()
131:             .await
132:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("AWS SecretsManager error: {e}"))?;
133: 
134:         let secret_string = resp
135:             .secret_string()
136:             .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("Secret {} not found", CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME))?;
137: 
138:         let secrets: Secrets = serde_json::from_str(secret_string)
139:             .map_err(|e| anyhow!("Failed to parse consolidated secret: {e}"))?;
140: 
141:         self.cache
142:             .insert(CONSOLIDATED_SECRET_NAME.to_string(), secrets.clone());
143:         Ok(secrets)
144:     }
145: }

149:     async fn get(&self, key: SecretKey) -> Result<String> {
150:         if let Ok(val) = self.env.get(key).await {

152:         }

154:         let secrets = self.load_secrets().await?;
155:         match key {
156:             SecretKey::DatabaseUrl => Ok(secrets.database_url),
157:             SecretKey::TaskApiKey => Ok(secrets.task_api_key),
158:             SecretKey::CaptchaSecret => Ok(secrets.captcha_secret),
159:             SecretKey::GoogleCreds => Ok(secrets.google_creds),
160:             SecretKey::EmailAccounts => Ok(secrets.email_accounts),
161:             SecretKey::SepaCreditorName => Ok(secrets.sepa_creditor_name),
162:             SecretKey::SepaCreditorIban => Ok(secrets.sepa_creditor_iban),
163:             SecretKey::SessionSecret => Ok(secrets.session_secret),
164:         }
165:     }
166: }

content.text:
Done. SEPA XML now matches the working bank-accepted file structure.

## Changes
- `src/logic/secrets.rs` — new `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` secret (env key + struct field + getter)
- `src/logic/banking.rs:generate_sepa_xml` — added `creditor_id` param, plus:
  - `xsi:schemaLocation` on `<Document>`
  - `<CreDtTm>` Z-suffix (UTC)
  - `<BtchBookg>true</BtchBookg>`
  - `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>` (was hardcoded `FRST`)
  - `<CtgyPurp><Cd>OTHR</Cd></CtgyPurp>`
  - `<Ccy>EUR</Ccy>` in `<CdtrAcct>`
  - `<CdtrSchmeId>` block with `PrvtId/Othr/Id` + `SchmeNm/Prtry=SEPA` (the **main fix**)
  - `<AmdmntInd>false</AmdmntInd>` in every `<MndtRltdInf>`
- `src/logic/events.rs:export_sepa_xml` — reads `SepaCreditorId`, extends `ConfigIncomplete` check, passes through to generator
- `.env.example` — `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID=` added
- Tests extended with assertions for all new elements; XSD validation still passes

## Verification
- `cargo build` ✓ (with `SQLX_OFFLINE=true`, since `DATABASE_URL` in `.env` points to unreachable host `pi`)
- `cargo test banking` ✓ 3/3 (includes XSD validation)
- `cargo test secrets` ✓ 1/1
- `cargo clippy --all-features` ✓ no issues
- `cargo fmt` ✓ no changes

## Action required from you
1. Set `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` in your local `.env` (the value from the working file is `DE54ZZZ00000299406` — that's the legal SEPA Creditor Identifier assigned by the Bundesbank, you should already have it)
2. Add `SEPA_CREDITOR_ID` to the AWS consolidated secret in Secrets Manager (same value) so the Lambda can read it
3. Re-export a SEPA file via the API and confirm

Note: `<SeqTp>RCUR</SeqTp>` is hardcoded. For genuine first-time mandates (`FRST`) or one-off (`OOFF`), this will be wrong — the follow-up is to make it per-event or per-booking as discussed in the plan's open follow-up.
